data.title: Cogent announces IPv4 address securitization offering
data.description:
• Cogent Communications Holdings (NASDAQ:CCOI) on Friday announced that a special-purpose, bankruptcy remote, indirect wholly owned subsidiary of the company has priced $174.4M aggregate principal amount of secured Internet Protocol version 4 ("IPv4") address revenue term notes, 6.646% Series 2025-1.
  • The notes will have an anticipated repayment term of five years.
  • Cogent intends to use the net proceeds of the offering for general corporate purposes.
  • Cogent expects the notes transaction to close on or around April 11, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Match says activist claims 'inaccurate and misleading'
data.description:
Match Group (NASDAQ:MTCH) on Friday said activist investor Anson Funds Management made “inaccurate and misleading characterizations” about the online dating service provider. 

Anson Funds Portfolio Manager Sagar Gupta earlier in the day said in a letter that it “has no choice but to publicly pursue refreshment through replacement. 

Anson said its private feedback to Match was “met with great resistance.” He called Match’s current board as “outdated, insular,” adding that it is “inadequately qualified to oversee a modern technology company primarily serving young adults.”

A Match spokesperson told Seeking Alpha that Anson’s letter “contains inaccurate and misleading characterizations of the company and our engagement with the investor.”

According to the spokesperson, Match’s board has “deep expertise and diverse perspectives across technology, consumer and financial sectors.”

Anson said it was nominating three candidates to Match’s board at the company’s annual shareholders meeting. They include Fumbi Chima, Laura Lee, and Kelley Morrell. Chima has been a technology executive at Adidas (OTCQX:ADDDF)(OTCQX:ADDYY), Fox Networks, and Walmart (WMT), while Lee has held content leadership positions with YoutTube and Twitch. Morrell was a board member of Bumble (BMBL), a Match rival. 

Match’s spokesperson said the board has reviewed the nominees in accordance with its governance policies. 

Anson said Match’s board “only considered” changes after “extreme shareholder pressure,” criticizing the panel for a series of CEO changes instead of resetting its ranks. In February, Zillow Group (Z)(ZG) co-founder Spencer Rascoff joined Match’s board last year after talks with Elliott Investment Management. 

Rascoff, “in partnership with the management team and the board, is actively addressing key strategic priorities to drive sustainable growth and create long-term stockholder value,” the Match’s spokesperson said.

data.title: Beyond Oil signs distribution agreement with Mandarin Food Products in Netherlands
data.description:
• Beyond Oil (OTCQB:BEOLF) has signed its first distribution agreement in the Netherlands with Mandarin Food Products, the company said on Friday.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, which was signed March 25, 2025 and came into effect on the same day, Mandarin Foods will market, distribute, and sell Beyond Oil's proprietary oil filtration solution to restaurants, hotels, catering companies, and institutional food providers across the Netherlands.
  • The agreement includes an initial order of 5,400 kg of Beyond Oil's product and establishes a structured purchasing plan for orders that are expected to increase significantly each year.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sunnova Energy International receives non-compliance notice from NYSE
data.description:
• Sunnova Energy International (NYSE:NOVA) said on Friday that it was notified by the New York Stock Exchange that it is not in compliance with NYSE listing standards, which requires listed companies to maintain an average closing share price of at least $1.00 over a consecutive 30 trading-day period.
  • The company said it will closely monitor the closing share price of its common stock and is considering all available options and intends to regain compliance with the NYSE listing standards, including potentially through the consummation of a reverse stock split, subject to approval by the company’s board of directors and shareholders.
  • NOVA shares were down more than 1% in extended trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: B. Riley Financial receives Nasdaq delinquency notice
data.description:
• B. Riley Financial (NASDAQ:RILY) received an expected delinquency notification letter from Nasdaq that RILY was not in compliance with Nasdaq as a result of the delayed filing of the company's full year report.
  • RILY must submit a plan within 60 calendar days or June 2, 2025 to address how it intends to regain compliance with Nasdaq's listing rules.
  • If accepted, Nasdaq can grant an exception to up to 180 calendar days from the filing's due date, or until September 29, 2025, to regain compliance.
  • RILY shares fall 1.8% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: AIM ImmunoTech receives NYSE American notice of delisting and appeal
data.description:
• AIM ImmunoTech (NYSE:AIM) said on Friday that it has received notification from NYSE American that it had suspended trading of the company’s common stock and of its determination to commence delisting proceedings due to the low selling price of the company’s common stock. 
  • The company plans to appeal the determination to the exchange’s Listing Qualifications Panel. 
  • The company’s common stock is expected to begin trading on the Pink Open Market on April 7, 2025.
